QueensCollege.body: The system would automatically enroll students to the class who are from the waitlist would be in the order of its received.

Drop a course for 100% refund is August 25

Drop a course for 75% refund is September 1

Drop a course for 50% refund is September 8

Drop A course for 25% refund is September 15

A grade of W is assigned beginning September 16

Last day to withdraw with a grade of W is November 6

All dates listed you have until 11:59 pm eastern time to do it.

TAP, PELL, and Excelsior isn’t up to the CUNY. It’s up to the state and federal government to decide if you get any aid based on how much your parents earn for that reported year. Even if you go to another CUNY, it might’ve been the same case.

They have the deadline of the 27th-29th to input all their grades. Some started their break early by having to travel for the holidays and couldn’t put in the grades. Since Christmas Eve and Christmas has just passed, it’s likely professors are just starting to put in grades or finishing up grading the assignments and such. Good luck!

CUNY.body:
Your school should have an emergency fund, look for it first. They may also have a food pantry and connections to further resources.

Then connect with other resources that NYC and nonprofits may offer regarding work and housing assistance.

As much as schools hate it, the website definitely helped me choose the right classes... after you filter out the angry comments.

As with any review-based system, hopefully users are smart enough to analyze reviews for content.

If specific reviews are angry with all caps and nonsensical, you shouldn't give them much weight. When there's actual criticism that's detailed in a rational tone, you factor that into your decision making.

Problem is, transfer credits don't affect your GPA. Your Hunter GPA is still waiting for you if you were to be approved for readmission.

If you are somehow approved on your appeal for readmission, using the CUNY F grade repeat policy would help boost your GPA... If you haven't already taken those courses at your new school. 

But, realistically, this would be an uphill battle to get readmitted back, and then to meet the major requirements for entry. You may need to consider some other school...

CUNY.body: The PELL Grant cuts are gonna go into effect next year; July 2026. Also the 15 credits for full time status and the max PELL Grant slash was gutted from the final bill the House passed, so no worries. Full time status will still be 12 and max PELL stays the same.

When ur on academic probation u can only take 13 credits, or else you will have issues later. I suggest you take 4 classes; 12 credits, that are not too difficult to pass, as the goal for the Fall semester is to bring ur GPA up as high as possible, and regain ur financial aid. Also, please look at your college’s probation policy as it varies depending on your college and how many credits you are allowed to take while on probation.
Poor academic performance is the reason why u would be dismissed.

HunterCollege.body: 100% scam. Complete scam.  That email has achieved maximum scamminess. Delete it with extreme prejudice.

Short answer is no. Absolutely not. 

You can NOT be required to turn on your camera in class or in an exam unless there is a real and compelling pedagogical reason for the requirement (the professor’s desire to catch cheaters or prevent cheaters is NOT a good enough reason. 

The longer policy answer is on this page [https://www.cuny.edu/coronavirus/academic-continuity/guidance-on-academic-continuity-to-campuses/](https://www.cuny.edu/coronavirus/academic-continuity/guidance-on-academic-cont...
Just go ahead and get vaccinated. Why in the world would you wait?

QueensCollege.body:
When you get notified you have one week to get the random test. During that week you have full access to campus, no problem. 

You can get the test at any cuny campus, anywhere in the city. You walk in and do it and you’re out in five minutes. Usually no waiting at all. Results usually take about 24-48 hours (in most cases closer to 24). 

If you don’t go for the test and get a negative result, THEN (a full week after you were notified) you are blocked from campus. 

So if you got notified, a...

Professors can not require that you keep your cameras on for tests or during class. 

See this page

https://www.cuny.edu/coronavirus/academic-continuity/guidance-on-academic-continuity-to-campuses/

Where you will find this:
—
Note: It is important to recognize that students may not be compelled to (1) turn on their cameras during test-taking in web conferencing digital technology platforms like Zoom or 2) agree to the terms and conditions of proctoring solutions procured by the University, ...

It would be incredibly stupid, but it's not true.  That does NOT happen.  


They notify you that you need to be randomly tested, and they give you a week, 7 days, to get the test.  During those 7 days, you are free to come on campus and not locked out.   


The only way you'll miss your midterm is if you ignore the notice, wait until the very last minute, and get your test on day 6 or 7.  If you're that stupid, you deserve a 0 for your exam.

If you belong in college (meaning, you know how t...

BrooklynCollege.body: Colleges don’t charge for commencement tickets. No cost to you. Each student can request up to four tickets at Brooklyn’s commencement this year.

The parking lot at Target is quite close to campus and not too expensive. Always has space in my experience although you might have to go up to the top level in busy shopping season. Bonus is that there’s a Starbucks in the Target and you can pick up some other things you need while you’re there. 

Two hours parking is only $3, but it jumps up to $10 for three hours and $20 for eight hours. Still probably cheaper than LIRR plus subway (maybe?). 

You can find street or metered parking sometim...

CUNY.body: Another place to consider is Baruch (yes, Baruch).  They don't get a lot of Biology majors (most of the students major in business) so the faculty cherish each one.  Their premeds get a lot of individual attention, & Baruch has a great record of getting their students into good health professions graduate schools.

Anyone thinking of transferring to Hunter should watch and think carefully about how their credits will transfer.  This tool, [Transfer Explorer](https://explorer.lehman.edu/), will tell you how any CUNY course is supposed transfer to any other CUNY college.  Hunter has a long list of types of courses for which it will not give transfer credit (see link on [this page](http://www.hunter.cuny.edu/admissions/admissions-information/transfer-1/transfer#credit-transfer)) but know that it is CUNY po...
Baruch has few premeds and so give extensive individual support to them. They have an excellent record of sending their students on to graduate schools in the health professions.

Baruch.body: Baruch has major course requirements and also prerequisite course requirements for those major courses and courses required just to be admitted to the business school (over and beyond what is required to be admitted to Baruch). It also has extra requirements for general education courses, and has a history of not being generous in giving out transfer credit (compared to some other CUNY colleges). All of which could result in your needing *significant* extra time to finish your bachelor's. Thi...

A follow up to my earlier comment:  See bookmark link below from Transfer Explorer for all courses across CUNY that transfer as ACC 2203 (those that are recently or currently being offered).

[https://explorer.lehman.edu/bookmarks/ILh4tjpZTl](https://explorer.lehman.edu/bookmarks/ILh4tjpZTl)

Any course taken at any CUNY college is supposed to give you at least elective credit at any other CUNY college. The specific kind of credit you will get is shown in the [Transfer Explorer](https://explorer.cuny.edu/) tool. If a CUNY college will not give at least elective credit for a credit-bearing course taught at another CUNY college, then you have the right to appeal (and you will win that appeal). The appeals process is [here.](https://www.cuny.edu/about/administration/offices/undergrad...

HunterCollege.body: Students should consider CUNY's Baruch College for prehealth undergrad. Yes, Baruch is primarily a business school, but they have a great Bio major and a great record of getting students into graduate health programs. There aren't many prehealth major students at Baruch, so the faculty cherish all of them and give them lots of support.
Macaulay Honors College at Hunter is extremely prestigious and difficult to get into, students are extremely well supported in all ways, and students have outstanding peers with whom to interact.

Your situation sounds unusual so this may not apply to you, but Hunter takes virtually no transfer students into nursing. I strongly suggest you check with the Nursing Department about this before you go any further.

QueensCollege.body: The [Transfer Explorer](https://explorer.cuny.edu/) tool can tell you how a nonQueens course will transfer to Queens in terms of the credit it will get, but I believe that, even if Transfer Explorer tells you that you will get the sort of credit you need for your Queens major, the department can still not allow you to count the e-permitted course for your major, so check with the department.

Getting your AP classes to count for college credit is essentially a transfer credit process (i.e., it's similar to when you transfer from one college to another and want the second college to count your credits from the first college).  If you aren't satisfied with how your AP credits are being counted (or not) towards your degree requirements in college, you can appeal that decision.  Here is the procedure for appealing transfer credit decisions at CUNY:  [https://www.cuny.edu/about/adminis...
I'm not understanding what you're saying.  A 3-credit course should be no problem because 42 is divisible by 3.  So you can do the 42 credits by taking 14 3-credit courses.

QueensCollege.body:
The professor is wrong. The CUNY central mandated policy is here:  [https://www.cuny.edu/coronavirus/credit-no-credit-policy/](https://www.cuny.edu/coronavirus/credit-no-credit-policy/) 

​

The relevant section is here: 

> if a student chooses to exercise this option, a passing letter grade (A, B, C, or D) will convert to ‘CR’ with credit for the class being awarded, while a failing grade (F) will convert to ‘NC’, with no credit awarded. Credit/No Credit grades will not impact the st...

It's fake. An important indicator here is that the email address is name.nameNUMBER, faculty and staff emails don't use the number. Of course before any of that it's also a pretty obvious fake from the content.

Federal law requires that all colleges, including CUNY, disclose textbook and other paid requirements (like having to pay for Cengage) for each course section, so students aren't surprised by fees after signing up. 
CUNYFirst has a slot in each section display that is there for listing textbooks. But most departments have stopped filling it out and CUNY Administration doesn't do any follow up on it.

CUNY is badly out of compliance. The fact that you needed to make this worthy website is proo...

Baruch.body:
People are not reading and spreading false information assuming that if we don't fill out this form, we don't get the grant at all. That is not true whatsoever, people really need to learn how to read.

This form is completely OPTIONAL, if you don't fill out the form, you will just have the grant be refunded to you. BUT if you do fill out the form, you are GRANTING CUNY PERMISSION to use your heerf funds towards your balance/charges that are on your cunyfirst account.

HunterCollege.body:
if your vaccine was approved on cunyfirst, you can just use your id to scan and no line. there is a really long line for those who don't have id, don’t have their vaccine approved and/or are not vaccinated yet. 

they didn't check the vaxpass since our onecards are already updated once we are approved on cunyfirst.
I think they offer media 180 in the winter! If you need to retake it, I recommend taking it in the winter semester. I took it last winter and it was an Easy A for me.
If you are approved on cunyfirst, your hunter one card ID will update and you can easily enter campus.

QueensCollege.body: Check FACTS, it will tell you if you are eligible or ineligible for TAP.

Are you on academic probation right now? If you are not, you can apply for federal loans that can help pay your tuition (no interest until after 5 months you graduate). 

But if you are, then all grants and financial aid are suspended until your gpa goes back up to 2.0+. I would try speaking out to your family members to see if they can help. I would not advise private loans, but if that is the only option you have, then try looking into Discovery or Navient.

I recommend looking into federal loans provided from fafsa. those are much better than private student loans as well. 

you can also speak to financial aid in person, i feel in person is the best option, and there should be someone out there in the dept willing to help. :(

CUNY.body: So it sounds like it has nothing to do with you being an applicant. These pages just follow everyone who follows them.

HunterCollege.body:
It rocks!

(Sorry, I couldn’t resist.)
Yes, if clearly stated in the syllabus.

It’s a perfectly fine midrange college for psychology. You likely are already aware that Hunter, as a CUNY school, primarily serves NYC commuter students. That means that there’s not much campus life. Most students just go to classes and then head home or to work. Also, you’ll likely pay considerably more as an out of state student. Whether it’s “right” for you will depend in large part why you want to be in NYC.

QueensCollege.body: Looks like a phishing attempt.
Announced several weeks ago that it will be the same as this semester, with the vast majority of classes online.

Sorry to hear about the midterm - that’s an awful feeling.  Regarding CR/NC option, we just don’t know yet. On one hand, they created a precedent last semester in offering the option because all courses were online. On the flip side, there is going to be pressure not to offer it for a third semester in a row, as it would have long term academic impacts for students and the institution. (Interestingly, they didn’t offer it for the Summer & Winter sessions between.) If they decide to offer it, ...
